title: Pseudolysimachion longifolium
taxonomy: Pseudolysimachion longifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudolysimachion, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Lysimachia. Pseudolysimachion longifolium is a perennial herb that grows in moist environments, typically in mountainous regions. The plant is native to Europe, specifically in the mountainous areas of the Balkan Peninsula. Its classification is also sometimes placed in the genus Lysimachia due to similarities in its characteristics.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it exhibits potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases.
